title: Constants, Literals, Domain Types, and Configuration · Mastery Check
summary: Replace ambiguous magic values without creating meaningless constant noise. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a domain constant and configuration policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish harmless local literals from repeated domain rules, units, limits, and protocol values.
Name stable domain values by meaning and keep them near their owning policy.
Use enums, unions, literal types, branded values, or value objects for closed domains.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
